per_bead.note: Seam at gitops.go:22 (var execCommand = exec.Command). Production builds cmd := execCommand(...) then (post-fix) sets cmd.Env then .CombinedOutput()/.Output(). The stub RETURNS the *exec.Cmd that production mutates, so a TEST-ONLY extension that retains the returned cmd pointers (or adds an env field read after the call) can inspect .Env. No gitutil op sets env today -> GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT=0 assertion fails now, passes after. Hermetic; no live git. The plan explicitly flags the seam extension is needed and it IS feasible (cmd is returned before .Env is set, exactly the seam shape required).

bdSchemaDriftRE (beads.go:326) anchored only on 'could not be found' -> new phrasings (no such column / unknown column / Error 1054) are RED now, true after. _UnrelatedFailureSkips string stays false (conservative broadening). Symlink-dedup machinery all present: fakeBdDir (beads_test.go:932), t.Setenv(PATH), os.Symlink, EvalSymlinks+seenResolved+seenDir guards (beads.go:391-416). checkMultipleBdOnPath(r, root) call shape matches existing tests.

SelectWorkByName (select.go) uses exact item.ID == name today, so SelectWorkByName(items,"xxxx") for ID "mindspec-xxxx" errors now -> RED, passes after suffix match. Pure function, no seam needed. Existing TestSelectWorkByName_NamedInSetNotFirst / _NamedNotInSet patterns provide the no-fall-through-to-items[0] guard shape to extend.

SetListJSONForTest / listJSONFn seam (cache_test.go uses it). Stub signature func(args ...string) receives full argv including --status=open,in_progress,closed (cache.go:195). Captured arg lacks 'blocked' today -> argv-breadth assertion (contains blocked / bead.AllStatuses) is genuinely RED, GREEN after. bead.AllStatuses exists (config.go:456). Single comma-joined call keeps TestCache_GetChildren_MemoizesPerEpic (1 call/epic) green. Plan correctly notes the stub short-circuits before real config read so it is root-resolution-independent.

summary: All four RED tests are genuine (fail before, pass after) and hermetic with machinery that exists in the cited files. R1's flagged risk -- env capture -- is viable: execCommand returns the *exec.Cmd that production mutates with .Env, so a test-only seam extension that retains the returned cmd (or adds an env field) can assert GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT=0; no op sets env today so the assertion is RED now. R4's argv capture is inspectable via the listJSONFn variadic-args stub (the --status= arg is captured and lacks 'blocked' today). R2 (bdSchemaDriftRE table + fakeBdDir/os.Symlink/Setenv PATH dedup) and R3 (pure SelectWorkByName) need no new seam. No non-hermetic or unwritable test found.
